micro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's Expansion: Prohibiting Even Maintenance of Kilayim, Not Just Planting

topical_tags.term: Beit Hashalchin (irrigated field requiring regular water)
Beit Ha'aal (rain-fed field not requiring regular irrigation)
Davar Ha'aved / Hefsed (financial loss or damage prevention)
Tircha (excessive effort or labor)
Chol HaMoed (intermediate festival days—permitted and prohibited labors)
Shvi'is / Shemita (Sabbatical year restrictions)
Rashi vs. Rabbeinu Gershom (authorship and manuscript traditions)
Rif and Ran (halakhic codifiers and commentaries)
Rabbi Meir (lenient position allowing irrigation even for profit)
Rabbi Yehuda (middle position limiting to loss-prevention cases)
Rabbi Elazar ben Azariah (stringent position prohibiting irrigation)
Meniat Revach (prevention of profit vs. prevention of loss)
Choresh (plowing or ground-working as melacha category)
Zore'a (planting as melacha category)
Kilayim (mixed planting—prohibited combinations)
Rabbi Akiva (position on melacha and kilayim maintenance)
Aveilus (mourning laws and restrictions)
Masechet Megilla (parallel debates on tract ordering)
Tsarchei Rabim (public welfare needs)
Ma'ayan (spring or wellspring source)
Ametha / Amah (irrigation canal or furrow)
Rav Papa (gemarah interpreter attributing mishnah positions)
Rav Huna (identifier of mishnah authorship)
Rabbah (position on melacha classification by physical form)
Rav Yosef (position on melacha classification by purpose/intent)
Abaaye (questioner challenging both melacha positions)
Tosafot (rishon commentary on melacha categorization)
